mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding economic activity and stronger resource utilization, which pushes up real interest rates and inflation expectations, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation between industrial production and yields
failure_condition: If the 60-month rolling correlation between INDPRO monthly change and next 20-day yield change is below 0.1 or if Rank IC over any 3-year window is less than 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.5%, as it is now (0.09%), the market anticipates eventual monetary policy normalization, so long-term yields will rise over the next 60 trading days as expectations of tighter policy build.
evidence_refs: fact:forward guidance and market pricing of rate hikes in 2019
failure_condition: If after a trigger instance, the 10-year yield does not rise by at least 50 basis points over the next 60 trading days in at least 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When both CPIAUCSL and INDPRO are rising simultaneously, it signals demand-pull inflation and rising capacity utilization, which forces the market to demand higher term premiums, pushing the 10-year yield upward over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Phillips Curve and production cycle linkages
failure_condition: If on trading days where both series are above their 12-month moving averages, the forward 20-day yield change is not positive more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During the ongoing post-recession recovery regime, a monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) greater than 2% indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which raises real economic activity and pushes yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:housing-led recovery phases in post-2009 era
failure_condition: If when HOUST monthly change > 2% and the economy is in a recovery phase (real GDP growth positive for 2 consecutive quarters), the 20-day forward yield change is positive less than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates labor market strength and wage growth expectations, which feeds into inflation expectations and raises the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation between employment growth and yields
failure_condition: If the 60-month rolling correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and next 20-day yield change is below 0.1 or Rank IC over any 3-year window falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below the estimated natural rate of 4.5%, it signals labor market tightness and upward wage pressure, leading to higher yields over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE crosses below 4.5%, the 20-day forward yield change is not positive at least 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 1%), a rising CPI (CPIAUCSL monthly change above 0.2%) reduces real yields and forces investors to demand higher nominal yields to compensate for inflation, causing the 10-year yield to increase over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:real rate channel in monetary policy
failure_condition: If on dates satisfying the joint trigger, the 20-day forward yield change is not positive in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong retail sales (RSAFS above 400,000), consumer spending is robust, indicating above-potential demand that raises inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year yield higher over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:historical threshold of 400B for RSAFS indicating strong consumption
failure_condition: If on days with RSAFS above 400,000, the 5-day forward yield change is positive less than 55% of the time.
